Looking at the function, the docstring says an entry is valid "while `now` is strictly before the expiration boundary `cached_at + ttl`". This means the entry should expire when `now >= cached_at + ttl`.

The current condition on line 72 is `if now > cached_at + validated_ttl`, which means entries are still returned when `now == cached_at + ttl` (i.e., exactly at expiration). This contradicts the specification that says "strictly before" the expiration boundary.

The correct condition should be `if now >= cached_at + validated_ttl` to properly expire entries at the boundary.
